headlines.title: Starmer couldn’t master the media. Can Burnham?
headlines.snippet: Keir Starmer's rapid fall from power is attributed to his inability to master the media, reflecting a broader trend of leadership changes in Britain.

headlines.title: US and Iran trade strikes days after peace agreement
headlines.snippet: The US and Iran have exchanged strikes just days after signing a memorandum of understanding, signaling a breakdown in recent peace efforts.

headlines.title: Serbia’s protesters press on even after Vucic promises to step aside
headlines.snippet: Serbian protesters continue their demonstrations despite President Vucic's promise to step aside, expressing skepticism about his genuine surrender of power after 12 years.

headlines.title: Why do reports of domestic abuse increase during World Cups?
headlines.snippet: Reports of domestic abuse tend to increase during World Cups for reasons that are explored in this news item.

headlines.title: In Lebanon, framework agreement signed with Israel spurs protest, criticism
headlines.snippet: A framework agreement signed between Lebanon and Israel has sparked widespread protest and criticism due to its failure to mandate Israeli troop withdrawal from occupied land, following months of deadly attacks.

headlines.title: Gaza dialysis patients at risk as machines fail
headlines.snippet: Gaza's health system is crippled by Israel's blockade and repeated attacks, leading to nearly half of the dialysis machines at the largest hospital failing. A shortage of crucial medical supplies, like sodium bicarbonate, has forced 25 machines out of service, leaving only 26 partially operational.

headlines.title: Israeli forces push deeper into southern Syria
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces have expanded their presence in Syria’s southern Deraa province, establishing new checkpoints and moving military vehicles deeper into the region. Syrian state news agency SANA reported the installation of a second checkpoint near Sreyeh Jamlah and troop movements into the village of Abidin.

headlines.title: Qatari citizen killed by shrapnel after vessel goes missing
headlines.snippet: A Qatari citizen has been killed by shrapnel from military operations in the area where a maritime vessel went missing. Qatar's Ministry of Interior confirmed the death while searching for the vessel, also reporting another person wounded.

headlines.title: Tehran-Dubai flights to restart after Israeli-US war
headlines.snippet: Flights between Tehran and Dubai are scheduled to resume on Monday, according to Iranian media. This reopening follows months of disruption to air travel in the Gulf region after the Israeli-US war.

headlines.title: Israel took wounded Palestinian from hospital; 18 months later, family told he is dead
headlines.snippet: The family of Majdi Nour Abu Ara was informed 18 months after Israeli forces bombed his vehicle that he had been killed. Israeli authorities had concealed his fate since the drone strike in the occupied West Bank on December 3, 2024.
